data.reasoningText:
The user has migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference - Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns a non-zero exit code (8) when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated this as a success.

Let me look at their project files to understand the setup.

data.content:
The issue is clear. Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns **exit code 8** ("no tests found") when zero tests are discovered, unlike VSTest which returned 0. Since `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` isn't defined, all tests are compiled out.

The fix is a single MSBuild property:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat assemblies with zero tests as success (exit 0 instead of 8). -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. With Microsoft.Testing.Platform (enabled via `EnableMSTestRunner`), when no tests are discovered (because `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` is not defined), the runner returns exit code 8 (which means "no tests found"). VSTest used to return 0 in this case.

The fix is to set the MSBuild property `<TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>` which tells the platform to return exit code 0 when no tests are found instead of exit code 8.

Let me also verify this by building and running.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat assemblies with zero tests as success (exit 0 instead of 8). -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

data.reasoningText:
The log reveals two important things:

1. `--minimum-expected-tests` requires a "non-zero positive integer" - so 0 is not allowed.
2. There's a `--ignore-exit-code` option: "Do not report non successful exit value for specific exit codes (e.g. '--ignore-exit-code 8;9' ignore exit code 8 and 9 and will return 0 in these case)"

So the correct fix is to use `--ignore-exit-code 8` instead.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Treat "no tests discovered" (exit code 8) as success. -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests without failing (exit 0 instead of 8). -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
**The fix:** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns **exit code 8** when no tests are discovered (VSTest returned 0). Adding `--ignore-exit-code 8` via the `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments` MSBuild property tells the runner to treat that as success.

- ✅ Zero tests → exit code **0**
- ✅ All tests pass → exit code **0**  
- ✅ Test failures still produce a non-zero exit code

If you have multiple test projects, you can put this property in a `Directory.Build.props` to apply it solution-wide.
